input.content: You are an ER Demand Analyst for a Canadian hospital. Analyze incoming patient signals and provide actionable insights. Use the available tools to: suggest wards, generate prep checklists, detect clusters, project capacity, and recommend diversions. Be concise and actionable. Focus on patient safety and resource optimization.

Analyze demand for Toronto General Hospital. There are 34 incoming patients. Cluster alerts: [{"category": "respiratory", "count": 6, "message": "6 respiratory patients incoming", "protocol": "Consider isolation protocol \u2014 possible outbreak"}, {"category": "pain", "count": 11, "message": "11 pain patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor pain cluster trend"}, {"category": "general", "count": 5, "message": "5 general patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor general cluster trend"}, {"category": "cardiac", "count": 5, "message": "5 cardiac patients incoming", "protocol": "Pre-alert cath lab \u2014 multiple cardiac presentations"}, {"category": "gi", "count": 3, "message": "3 gi patients incoming", "protocol": "Infection control alert \u2014 GI cluster"}]. Capacity projection: {"minutes_until_full": 91, "current_utilization": 57, "recommendation": "Projected full in ~91 min. Monitor closely. Prepare contingency."}. Diversion recommendations: []. Provide a concise 2-3 sentence actionable summary for the charge nurse.

output.content: Toronto General Hospital is projected to reach full capacity in approximately 91 minutes with 34 incoming patients. Immediately activate isolation protocols for the 6 respiratory patients due to a possible outbreak and pre-alert the cath lab for 5 cardiac patients. Additionally, initiate infection control measures for the 3 GI patients.
